root.name: Task Completion: Make fourth and fifth items in numbered list on slide 12 consistent with first three
root.description: Evaluates whether the agent successfully made the fourth and fifth items in the numbered list on slide 12 look consistent with the first three items, without making unwanted changes elsewhere

children.name: Attempt Detection
children.description: Critical check to verify that some attempt was made to modify the fourth and fifth items in the numbered list on slide 12

children.name: Consistency Achievement
children.description: Evaluates how well the fourth and fifth items in the numbered list were made consistent with the first three items using visual assessment

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    # Get slide 12 screenshot
    slide_12_screenshot = None
    for screenshot in modified_ppt_screenshots:
        if screenshot.slide_number == 12:
            slide_12_screenshot = screenshot
            break
    
    if slide_12_screenshot is None:
        return "Slide 12 not found in screenshots", 0.0
    
    prompt = """Look at this PowerPoint slide and examine the numbered list items. 
    
I need you to evaluate whether items 4 and 5 in the numbered list look visually consistent with items 1, 2, and 3 in terms of:
    - Font formatting (size, style, color, bold/italic)
    - Text alignment and indentation
    - Bullet/numbering style
    - Spacing and layout
    - Any other visual formatting elements
    
Please provide a detailed analysis of the consistency and rate it on a scale of 0-1 where:
    - 1.0 = Items 4 and 5 are completely consistent with items 1-3
    - 0.8-0.9 = Very consistent with only minor differences
    - 0.6-0.7 = Mostly consistent with some noticeable differences
    - 0.4-0.5 = Somewhat consistent but several formatting differences
    - 0.2-0.3 = Inconsistent with major formatting differences
    - 0.0-0.1 = Completely inconsistent or items not found
    
Respond with your analysis followed by 'SCORE: X.X' where X.X is your numerical rating."""
    
    response = vlm_call(prompt, [slide_12_screenshot.image_path], temperature=0.3)
    
    # Extract score from response
    score_line = [line for line in response.split('\n') if 'SCORE:' in line.upper()]
    if score_line:
        try:
            score = float(score_line[-1].split(':')[-1].strip())
            score = max(0.0, min(1.0, score))  # Clamp between 0 and 1
            return f"VLM assessment: {response}", score
        except:
            pass
    
    # Fallback scoring based on keywords in response
    response_lower = response.lower()
    if 'completely consistent' in response_lower or 'fully consistent' in response_lower:
        return f"VLM assessment indicates high consistency: {response}", 0.9
    elif 'very consistent' in response_lower or 'mostly consistent' in response_lower:
        return f"VLM assessment indicates good consistency: {response}", 0.7
    elif 'somewhat consistent' in response_lower:
        return f"VLM assessment indicates partial consistency: {response}", 0.5
    elif 'inconsistent' in response_lower:
        return f"VLM assessment indicates inconsistency: {response}", 0.2
    else:
        return f"VLM assessment unclear: {response}", 0.5

children.name: No Extraneous Changes
children.description: Verifies that no unwanted changes were made to other slides or to text content on slide 12

children.name: No Changes to Other Slides
children.description: Ensures that slides other than slide 12 were not modified

children.name: No Text Content Changes on Slide 12
children.description: Ensures that only formatting/styling was changed on slide 12, not the actual text content

metadata.task: On slide 12, make the fourth and fifth items in the numbered list look consistent with the first three. Context for rubric design: 1. Critical node, checking if at least some attempt seems to have been made to make the fourth and fifth items in the numbered list look consistent with the first three. 2. Non-critical node, use VLM to check with partial grading to check that the fourth and fifth items in the numbered list look consistent with the first three. 3. Non-critical, make sure no changes to other slides or text content changes to slide 12.
